💸 On the SecondFi exploit  The explanation going around reads like technical language assembled to look like an answer.   On the "weak RNG" root cause. A weak random number generator collapsing the keyspace is the stated cause. It fails as a complete explanation on two counts.  1. It does not explain the wallets that were generated in other apps, imported into SecondFi, and then drained. Those wallets should have been safe.  2. If weak RNG were the whole story, the correct response would be the opposite of "sit tight." Your existing seed is compromised, so the right move is to generate a brand-new wallet in a separate, reputable app and move your funds to it now, before the attacker sweeps the address.   On "the vkey is revealed only when you spend."  If you have ever sent a single transaction from an address, its public key is already on-chain and trivially retrievable from Koios or any indexer. The vkey is deterministic per address and identical across every transaction that address ever signs. A transaction today reveals nothing that was not already public the first time you spent.  On "sweeping every address the moment it moves."  A new transaction publishes no new on-chain information beyond its own signature, and that signature is deterministic Ed25519. It uses no RNG, and any compliant wallet produces the identical signature for the same transaction. Given that this incident affects only SecondFi users and no other wallet, signing or broadcasting any new transaction does not increase your exposure.  My read, pending fuller disclosure.  Information is being withheld. Emurgo has not shared enough to explain what actually happened, and the public root-cause statement does not fit the full set of reported drains.   "Weak key generation" is at best incomplete. It cannot explain imported or migrated wallets being drained. That points to keys being compromised at the application level, not only at generation.  Any advice to stay put and wait does not protect users. For a non-custodial wallet, freezing balances and entering maintenance mode stops nothing on-chain. An attacker holding your private key is unaffected by the app being offline. Every hour spent waiting on a compromised seed is an hour the attacker can act in. This reads LESS like user protection and more like limiting their own fallout (prevent further loss of customers), and it leaves users more exposed, not less.  I will revise this if real technical detail is published. So far, nothing shared justifies leaving funds on a compromised seed and waiting to be drained.

NDLEA uncovers another industrial scale clandestine meth laboratory in Oyo forest  . Arrests Mexican, 4 others; recovers multi-billion-naira worth of illicit substances  . We’ll find you in the cities, track you into the forests, and dismantle your infrastructure of death, Marwa warns drug cartels  Operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) have struck yet another decisive blow against transnational drug syndicates following the discovery and dismantling of a fortified, industrial-scale clandestine methamphetamine laboratory operated by a Nigerian-Mexican cartel deep within the forest of Tapa Village, Ibarapa North Local Government Area, Oyo State.  The breakthrough comes barely four weeks after the Agency dismantled a similar massive meth laboratory in a forest in Ijebu East, Ogun State, signaling a desperate attempt by drug barons to turn the Southwest axis into a synthetic drug manufacturing hub.   Speaking on the development at the NDLEA Headquarters in Abuja, the Chairman/Chief Executive Officer of the Agency, Brig. Gen. Mohamed Buba Marwa (Rtd) who was represented by the Director of Media and Advocacy, Femi Babafemi at a press briefing on Wednesday 24th June 2026, commended the gallantry, dedication, and clinical professionalism of the officers involved in the operation.   According to him, “On Wednesday, 17th June 2026, tactical operatives of the NDLEA stormed a highly fortified, industrial-scale clandestine methamphetamine laboratory operating deep within the forest of Tapa Village, Ibarapa North Local Government Area, Oyo State.  “This was not a rudimentary setup; it was a sophisticated, highly organized transnational syndicate. During the raid, our operatives successfully arrested five key members of the cartel on-site. They include a 56-year-old Mexican Methamphetamine expert, Jose Villa Ochoa, brought in specifically to provide the technical expertise for large-scale synthesis and four Nigerian collaborators providing logistical support, cover, and local operations. They are: Maxwell Uche Nevoh, 30; Olatunji Yusuf, 37; Bankole Akeem Owolabi, 45; and ⁠Ganiu Monsiu, 43.  “The arrest of a foreign cartel specialist on Nigerian soil underscores the transnational nature of this threat, but more importantly, it underscores our Agency's world-class intelligence capability to track, intercept, and neutralize them. Following the successful raid, a specialized team from our Directorate of Forensic and Chemical Monitoring moved in on June 18th, 2026, to conduct a rigorous forensic examination of the facility. What they uncovered is a massive, factory-level production line of poison.  “The laboratory was fully stocked with a frightening array of precursor chemicals, industrial catalysts, and heavy-duty processing equipment. Chemicals and Materials recovered include: Phenyl-2-propanone (P2P), which is the the main, highly controlled precursor required for methamphetamine synthesis; 1800-litre drums containing Phenylacetic acid (the primary precursor used to synthesize P2P); Two 180L drums containing a staggering 300 litres of whitish crystalline substance; Four 180L drums containing dark liquid undergoing synthesis.  “Others include: 101 bags (25kg each) of Caustic Soda; 17 containers (25L each) of Sulphuric Acid; 19.5 containers (25kg each) of Tartaric Acid; 5 containers of Reniso Ultracool 68 (three 50L and two 25L capacity; 25 bottles (500ml each) of 80% Thioglycolic Acid; 2 containers (25L each) of Ethyl Phenylacetate, and 25 cartons of aluminum foil.  “The Industrial Processing Equipment discovered at the site include: One (1) Reactor Pot, which is the heart of the chemical synthesis; Two (2) mounted distillation units and three (3) fabricated mixers and condensers; as well as Two (2) vegetable dehydrator machines used for the rapid drying of the crystals.

An insight into how Britain is being Islamised is the career of Shabana Mahmood (شبانہ محمود in her native Urdu), the Pakistani Muslim who now occupies one of the most senior offices in the UK Government and, one day, may be the Prime Minister of the Islamic State of Britain.   Mahmood was born in Birmingham in 1980 to Pakistani Muslim parents who had emigrated to Britain in the late 1960s to early 1970s. When she was 1-year old, her family moved to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, where she lived for the next five years; so her formative years were spent in an absolute monarchy and Islamic state known world-wide for its abuses of human rights. These include prisoners being subjected to torture before facing rigged trials; its barbaric treatment of women, who under Islamic law must have a male guardian not of their own choosing; its violent enforcement of the barbarisms of Sharia law on its population, with offenders lashed, for example, for ‘insulting Islam’; its use of the death penalty for non-violent crimes; and for its violent suppression of free speech and protest.   Most weekends, the young Mahmood went with her family to Mecca, the holiest city in Islam, or to the mosques at Medina. On the family’s return to Birmingham, Mahmood’s father, who was born in Mirpur — the rape capital of Pakistan from which 60-80% of all Pakistani immigrants to England have come — became Chairman of the local Labour Party. This was in the early 2000s, when Labour had long given up representing the British working class and was cultivating its waning support from Britain’s immigrant and particularly Muslim population, which had grown exponentially under the replacement immigration policies of the New Labour Goverment of Tony Blair.   Shabana Mahmood went to Oxford University, where she studied politics and history before switching to law, and graduated with a 2nd-class Bachelor of Arts degree in jurisprudence. She then worked as a barrister in indemnity law for 1 year, and a further 3 years in insurance law. That’s the extent of her academic achievements and legal experience, which is very little for a future Secretary of State for Justice.   So how did this academically mediocre Pakistani Muslim girl rise to such a senior position? In 2010, Mahmood was elected MP for Birmingham Ladywood with 55.7% of the vote and a 10,105 majority on a 48.7% turnout. Together with Rushanara-Ali and Yasmin Qureshi, the MPs for, respectively, Bethnal Green and Bow and Bolton South East, this made Mahmood one of the first three female Muslim Members of Parliament. This was consistent with the Labour Party’s policy of promoting female Muslims to office in electoral constituencies with a large Muslim population. The population of the constituency of Ladywood is 3.7% Pakistani and 58.1% Asian, Black or other non-White ethnic groups; but 53.1% of all residents were Muslims in the 2021 Census. Unsurprisingly, it has an unemployment rate of over 40%. In 2011, a year into her office, and in her capacity as MP for Birmingham Ladywood, Mahmood said of her White constituents:   ‘The people you see holding the English flag, most of the time, down my neck of the woods, are mostly EDL [English Defence League]. And they are White, and they are male, and they’re bad people, and they want divide our communities from one another.’  In 2015, having been re-elected MP of this economically inactive population of Muslims with a vastly increased majority of 21,868, Mahmood was made Labour’s Shadow Chief Secretary to the Treasury, an office from which she subsequently resigned when Jeremy Corbyn was elected Labour Leader.   In January 2016, Mahmood was elected to represent the Parliamentary Party on the National Executive Committee, the governing body of the Labour Party; and that November she was elected as one of the vice chairs of Labour’s National Policy Forum.   At the snap 2017 election, Mahmood was again elected MP, again with an even further increased majority, 28,714 and 69.5% of the vote; as she was again in 2019, though with a slightly decreased majority of 28,582.     In 2018, Mahmood, together with 70 Islamic organisations operating in the UK, endorsed a report titled ‘Islamophobia Defined’. Published by the All Party Parliamentary Group on British Muslims (sic), this argued that:   ‘Islamophobia is rooted in racism and is a type of racism that targets expressions of Muslimness and perceived Muslimness.’  During the General Elections held in the UK since this Pakistani Muslim was elected a Member of the British Parliament, voter turnout in Mahmood’s constituency increased from 48.7% in 2010 to a high of 59% in 2017 before dropping to 56.12% in 2017; and it’s not hard to see where those votes are coming from in an electorate in which 53.1% of voters are Muslims. This is how identity politics works, and the Labour Party relies on and grants favours to the Muslim vote to gain office and political power.    As an example of such favours, in May 2021 Keir Starmer, now the Leader of the Labour Party, reappointed Mahmood to the Shadow Cabinet as Labour Party National Campaign Coordinator; and in 2023 he made her Shadow Secretary of State for Justice.   A devout Muslim, Mahmood, in an interview with Joseph Hayat, the editor-in-chief of British Muslim TV, in February 2024, openly declared the role of Islam in her life, as both a Muslim and a politican:   ‘Like a lot of practicing Muslims, my faith is the most important thing in my life. It is the absolute driver of everything that I do. I feel a very strong calling of my own conscience, and my conscience calls me to God.’  Indicative of where her allegiances lie, Mahmood said nothing about being driven by her commitment to UK legislation and the sovereignty of Parliament as its legislature, or about her conscience calling her to administer justice under its laws.   And if we thought this was a canny politician pandering to the Muslim electorate she hoped would return her to office, a month later, in March 2024, in an interview in the Sunday Times that announced her as the UK’s next Justice Secretary, Mahmood, sitting before a calligraphic depiction of the shahada, the proclamation of Islamic faith (‘I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of God’), repeated her credo:   ‘My faith is the centre-point of my life and it drives me to public service. It drives me in the way that I live my life and I see my life.’     Four months later, in the UK General Election, Mahmood was again elected MP for Birmingham Ladywood, but with a hugely reduced majority of just 3,421 on a 43.7% voter turnout. The message was clear: her Muslim electorate would vote her into office, but only if she represented their sectarian politics; and Keir Starmer’s support for the war in Gaza since October 2023 had drawn its voters away from the Labour Party and into the formation of another political force, formed around Muslim MPs loyal to the transnational Muslim diaspora or Ummah.    Undeterred, Keir Starmer, now Prime Minister of the UK, duly appointed Mahmood, with a grand total of three years as a junior barrister under her belt, the Secretary of State for Justice and Lord Chancellor. Her first act in office was to release 1,400 prisoners after 40% of their sentence had been served, clearing the UK’s prisons for what was to come. Eventually, she would oversee the early release of over 38,000 inmates from UK prisons in order to free up cells for protesters against the Islamisation of Britain.  This started, the same month she was appointed Justice Secretary, with the Southport terrorist attack in July 2024 by a second-generation Rwandan immigrant and Muslim who had been radicalised by an Al-Qaeda training manual. In response to this attack by an Islamic terrorist, Mahmood declared that ‘the full force of the law’ would be brought against the British people who had dared to protest against the murders, either on the street or online. She said nothing about the more than 40,000 jihadists on MI5’s watch list living in Britain. Instead, hundreds of Britons were sent to the now emptied prisons by the new Muslim Minister.     Mahmood’s remit as Justice Secretary encompassed justice policy for the whole of the UK, freedom of information, information privacy, human rights, civil liberties and miscarriages of justice. As a gesture to her waning support in the Muslim electorate, in the wake of the murderous attack on the children of White British families in Southport by an Islamic terrorist, Mahmood visited its mosque, she said, to show ‘solidarity’ with the religion of the terrorist.   But worse was to come. In the Cabinet reshuffle of September 2025, Mahmood was appointed the UK Home Secretary, an office previously held by the Duke of Wellington and Winston Churchill. In this capacity, her remit includes law enforcement in England and Wales, national security and counter terrorism, immigration and border security, and oversight of MI5, the UK’s domestic counter-intelligence and security agency.     This is despite the fact that, as of March 2024, 63% of prisoners in the UK arrested on terrorist-related charges are Islamic terrorists; and that MI5 currently lists Islamic terrorism as the most significant threat to Britain by volume. In effect, the keys to the security and safety of the British people, from both external and internal threats, has been placed in the hands of a follower of the ideology that presents the greatest threat to that security and safety. In both her stated beliefs and her actions as an MP and Minister, Mahmood is a jihadist, and she has been appointed to Home Secretary to oversee and bring about the Islamisation of Britain. If you would like to read more about how Britain is being Islamised, by whom and to what ends, and, more importantly, you want to know what we, the British people, can do to reclaim Britain from the control of jihadists like Mahmood and the globalists who control her, please consider purchasing a copy. 🚨 CONSOLIDATED CYBER INTELLIGENCE ALERT: ILLEGAL ACCESS CAMPAIGNS (DEFACEMENT / BACKDOORS) AND DENIAL-OF-SERVICE (DDoS) ATTACKS 🇺🇾 🇧🇷 🇦🇷 🇳🇮 🇪🇨 🇨🇱 🇨🇴 🇫🇷 🇮🇱 🇵🇱 🇺🇦 🇮🇩  [STATUS: MULTIPLE ACTIVE THREATS / PUBLIC PORTAL COMPROMISE / COORDINATED ATTACKS / DATE: JUNE 22, 2026]  :ONE CAMPAIGN RECORDED GLOBAL REPORT ON GOVERNMENT WEBSITE DEFIGURATION AND HIGH-IMPACT ATTACKS AGAINST CRITICAL IT INFRASTRUCTURE  Through the monitoring and correlation of cyber intelligence telemetry on open networks, a unified report is issued on multiple critical incidents recorded concurrently. The alert is divided into two main operational vectors: an international campaign targeting government websites, focused on deploying backdoors and defacement, and an increase in distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks and hacktivist interference directed at financial and governmental entities in Europe, the Middle East, and Latin America.  🏛️ SECTION I: MASSIVE INFILTRATION OF GOVERNMENT WEBSITES (ACTOR: PANATARAN)  The threat actor identified as Panataran has executed a vulnerability campaign aimed at compromising the perimeter of websites belonging to government institutions and highly relevant educational centers. By exploiting logical or code injection vulnerabilities, likely in Joomla technologies, the attacker managed to upload malicious files disguised as .gif and .txt files into temporary directories (/tmp/) and images (/images/), presumably using them as backdoors and persistence logs (defacement).  The list of targets compromised under Panataran's control is broken down below:  1. 🇺🇾 Uruguay — Ministry of the Interior Compromised Domain: anterior.minterior.gub.uy/tm…  2. 🇧🇷 Brazil — Information Technology Company of the State of Minas Gerais (Prodemge) Compromised Domain: wwws.prodemge.gov.br/images/…  3. 🇦🇷 Argentina — Judiciary of Formosa Compromised Domain: jusformosa.gob.ar/tmp/(redac…  4. 🇳🇮 Nicaragua — Nicaraguan Institute of Fisheries and Aquaculture (INPESCA) Compromised Domain: inpesca.gob.ni/images/(redac…  5. 🇪🇨 Ecuador — National Public Procurement Service and Local Governments Target 1: intranet.secap.gob.ec/images… (Professional Training Service)  Target 2: municipiodeatacames.gob.ec/i… (Decentralized Autonomous Government of Atacames)  Compromised Domain: genial-app.univ-lorraine.fr/… (University of Lorraine)  Compromised Domain: ppg-medesporte.unifesp.br/tm… (Federal University of São Paulo - Sports Medicine Postgraduate Program)  ⚔️ SECTION II: DENIAL-OF-SERVICE (DDoS) OPERATIONS AND RADICAL ACTIVISM  Concurrently, hacktivist and international cyber-warfare groups have launched massive traffic surges to disable critical government and banking infrastructure:  1. 🇫🇷 France — Local Governments (Actor: NoName057) The pro-Russian group NoName057 has launched the #opfrance campaign, attacking the municipal government portals of Gordes and Cassis. Availability reports confirm massive outages of citizen service portals due to a surge in logical requests (dead on ping).  2. 🇮🇱 Israel — Financial/Banking Sector (Actor: GORZ ROSTAM) Under the banner of the digital military campaign "OPERATION HAFT KHAN," the group GORZ ROSTAM launched volumetric DDoS attacks against the web platforms and online banking infrastructure of Israel Discount Bank (discountbank.co.il) and the First International Bank of Israel - FIBI (fibi.co.il), disrupting the availability of financial services.  3. 🇵🇱 Poland — Tax and Fiscal Applications (Actor: inteid) The collective inteid claimed responsibility for the total outage of the infrastructure behind e-pity. pl, one of Poland's most popular online applications and services for filing and processing personal tax returns. The attack disabled both the web applications and the mobile connection endpoints used for tax filing.  4. 🇨🇱 Chile — Government Entity (Actor: RSA CRACKERS) The hacktivist group RSA CRACKERS carried out a coordinated attack against the domain anci.gob.cl (associated with the Chilean government's perimeter administration), aiming to degrade connectivity to state portals.  5. 🇨🇴 Colombia — Local Hacktivist Cells (Actor: Anonymous Colombia)  Internal communications from the Anonymous Colombia cell have been detected on messaging channels, detailing the planning and structuring of imminent cyber-offensive operations within Colombian territory. Active surveillance remains in place regarding potential defacement or DDoS vectors targeting public sector perimeter infrastructure.  🛡️ GENERAL CONTAINMENT AND MITIGATION RECOMMENDATIONS (SOC)  🛑 Isolation and Removal of Injected Chains: Security teams at entities compromised by Panataran (Uruguay’s Ministry of the Interior, Formosa Judiciary, PRODEMGE, etc.) are urged to immediately isolate the identified web paths, remove malicious files (burka.gif, hayo.gif, al.txt), and conduct code integrity audits on file upload directories.  🔑 Closing Logical Gaps and Permission Verification: Review write policies on Apache and LiteSpeed ​​servers, blocking the ability to execute scripts or backend code from the `/images/` and `/tmp/` directories.  🛡️ Volumetric DDoS Mitigation: Activate perimeter-level protection rules on Web Application Firewalls (WAF) and implement traffic scrubbing via Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) to absorb massive request spikes targeting the banking and government entities under attack.  📊 MONITORING AND EVALUATION  Intelligence System: analyzer.vecert.io  Quickly assess your website's security at: monitor.vecert.io #CyberSecurity #GlobalThreats #Panataran #NoName057 #GorzRostam #RSACrackers #AnonymousColombia #Defacement #Backdoors #DDoS #GovernmentBreach #FintechSecurity #VECERT #Infosec #UnverifiedIncidents

This is the real shift in enterprise AI: not smarter prompts, but deeper context. The next generation of AI tools will not win because they can answer questions better. They will win because they understand the operating system of a company: decisions, documents, workflows, responsibilities and unfinished work. Brain² points to a future where AI is no longer a separate chat window, but an active layer inside the team itself. That can massively increase productivity, but it also raises serious questions about data governance, access control, privacy and trust. The companies that benefit most will not simply adopt AI. They will build the right structure around it.

In 1997, actor John C. McGinley’s son, Max, was born with Down syndrome. Shortly after, John's talent agent pulled him aside to deliver what was framed as practical advice: Do not talk about this publicly. Keep it quiet. People will stop hiring you.  For some, that might have sounded like reasonable career preservation. Protect the livelihood, avoid the spotlight, and pretend nothing had changed.  John’s response was immediate. He fired the agent.  Then, he did the exact opposite of what he had been told. He brought Max everywhere. Red carpets, talk shows, film sets, and public events. Wherever John went, Max was right beside him. At a time when society still largely preferred to keep individuals with developmental disabilities out of sight, John made a different choice. He made his son visible. Openly, proudly, and entirely without apology.  What began as a father's protective instinct grew into decades of fierce advocacy. John became one of the country's most recognizable voices for Down syndrome awareness. He spoke at global conferences, testified before Congress, and fought hard for employment law reforms that created real opportunities for people with disabilities to work, earn, and live independently.  During this journey, a reporter asked John a question that revealed far more about society's biases than it did about Max. The reporter asked if John ever wished his son were normal.  John didn't hesitate. He replied that Max was normal. The question wasn't. It was a blunt rejection of the idea that a person’s worth is measured by how well they fit into a narrow, conventional box.  Decades have passed since that conversation. Max is now 27 years old. He works, navigates his community, and lives an independent life filled with possibilities that the critics in 1997 never could have imagined for him.  Reflecting on their journey, John often says that Max never limited his life. He expanded it. Through his son, he learned what love, patience, and true commitment require.  The world signaled early on that it would have preferred Max to remain hidden in the shadows. John spent nearly three decades ensuring that the world looked Max right in the eye. Some fathers protect their children by shielding them from the world. Others protect them by refusing to let the world look away.  True inclusion begins when we stop treating differences as deficits. Max didn't need to change to fit into the world.   The world needed to change to make room for Max.

Dear @UNICEF,  Indonesian children should never be used to advance any political agenda or government campaign. Indonesia's Child Protection Law emphasizes that every child has the right to be protected from exploitation, violence, discrimination, and any activity that may jeopardize their best interests. Involving elementary school children in public rallies, asking them to carry campaign-style signs or chant political slogans raises serious concerns about whether their rights are being respected.  Children can’t meaningfully consent to political participation. Schools should remain safe spaces for learning—not places where students are mobilized to endorse government policies.  We urge independent monitoring to ensure that children's rights are fully protected and that no child is used as a tool for political messaging.

In a temporary shelter in Tupi, South Cotabato, silence is gradually being replaced by laughter and shared stories.  As part of our emergency response, ChildFund Philippines reached 32 children and 18 parents with psychosocial support, together with Ha Uman Association Inc., the Local Government of Tupi, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, and Radyo Kahiusa.  Through play, drawing, and guided activities led by facilitators from the Tupi Local Youth Development Office—who were trained by ChildFund and Ha Uman through the Climate Care Project supported by the European Union—children began to relax and engage. Parents and caregivers also joined sessions where they shared their fears, experiences, and hopes in the future.  Creating safe spaces like this helps children regain a sense of normalcy and supports families as they navigate their path ahead.  Because healing starts when we have a safe space to just be.  Be the lifeline they need as they journey toward recovery. sample_tweets.text: 🇫🇷 IMMO-GRATUIT.COM Allegedly Compromised via SQL Injection  A threat actor claims to have exploited a SQL injection vulnerability affecting immo-gratuit.com, a French real estate platform.  * According to the post, the actor released a partial database dump and states the extraction was intentionally limited due to the slow performance of the injection. * The sample allegedly contains user and account-related fields, including names, email addresses, passwords, phone numbers, agency information, session identifiers, and contact details. * The actor encourages others to continue extracting data from the vulnerable application, suggesting the issue may remain unpatched.  Analyst Note: Unlike many dark web posts that advertise previously obtained datasets, this claim describes an active exploitation path. If the vulnerability remains exposed, the risk extends beyond data leakage to potential ongoing unauthorized access, additional data extraction, account compromise, and further attacks against users and associated real estate agencies.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇻🇪 BANAVIH (Banco Nacional de Vivienda y Hábitat) Database Allegedly Offered for Sale  Threat actor group "L4T4MFUCK3R$" claims to be selling the complete BANAVIH database, associated with Venezuela's National Housing and Habitat Bank.  * The actors advertise approximately 54,000 folders totaling around 5.8 GB of data. * The dataset is reportedly being offered for sale rather than released publicly, with the group claiming buyers can request specific databases. * The post forms part of a broader campaign by the same threat actor group targeting Venezuelan government institutions.  Analyst Note: If authentic, a compromise of BANAVIH could expose sensitive citizen, financial, housing, and administrative records maintained by a government-backed institution. Such data could be leveraged for identity theft, fraud, social engineering, intelligence gathering, or targeting of government employees and beneficiaries of national housing programs.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Venezuela

🇲🇽 Mexico SALVAR & SEDENA Records Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor claims to have leaked data belonging to more than 5,000 patients from Mexico's SALVAR healthcare system and is advertising an additional dataset allegedly containing information on approximately 7,000 SEDENA (Mexican Army) applicants.  * The post includes a sample document appearing to contain sensitive medical information associated with HIV-related patient referral records. * According to the actor, the exposed data may include personal identifiers, health records, government-issued identifiers, and applicant-related information. * The leak appears to target both healthcare and government/military-linked records, significantly increasing the potential impact on affected individuals.  Analyst Note: If authentic, this is a highly sensitive exposure involving both protected health information (PHI) and data connected to military personnel applicants. Such datasets could be leveraged for identity theft, extortion, intelligence collection, targeted phishing, or personnel profiling. The inclusion of medical records substantially elevates the privacy and national security implications of the incident.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇻🇪 Ministry of Penitentiary Services of Venezuela Allegedly Breached  Threat actor group "L4T4MFUCK3R$" claims to have compromised a registration system belonging to Venezuela's Ministry of Popular Power for Penitentiary Services (MPPSP).  * The actors allege they obtained approximately 22,900 images and employee-related personal data from the affected system. * According to the post, the exposed information allegedly impacts personnel registered within the ministry's platform. * The threat actors framed the incident as part of an ongoing campaign targeting Venezuelan government entities.  Analyst Note: While the claims remain unverified, breaches involving prison and penitentiary systems can create significant operational and security risks. Exposure of employee identities, photographs, administrative records, or access-related information could facilitate targeted phishing, social engineering, intimidation campaigns, and broader intelligence collection against government personnel.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Venezuela

🇻🇳 Go2Joy Allegedly Leaked by RansomEXX – 1.4 Million Records Exposed  A threat actor claims to have leaked data from Go2Joy, a Vietnam-based travel and accommodation booking platform operating across Southeast Asia.  * The post states the leak was published by RansomEXX v2 on June 20, 2026. * According to the threat actor, the dataset contains approximately 1,398,693 customer records and 13,289 staff/partner records. * Allegedly exposed customer data includes:   * Password hashes   * Email addresses   * Mobile phone numbers   * Physical addresses   * Dates of birth   * Device fingerprints   * Loyalty balances   * Referral codes   * Booking histories * The actor further claims employee and partner records contain:   * Full names   * Corporate email addresses   * Role permissions   * Access-control information   * Operational and support-related contact data  Analyst Note: Travel platforms hold a unique combination of identity, contact, behavioral, and location-related information. If verified, exposure of booking histories and loyalty program data could enable highly targeted phishing campaigns, account takeover attempts, travel fraud, and broader intelligence collection on affected users.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Vietnam

🇫🇷 French Hospital Federation (FHF) Database Allegedly Leaked – 30,728 Records  A threat actor has publicly released a dataset allegedly associated with FHF.fr, the website of the French Hospital Federation (Fédération Hospitalière de France).  * The post claims the database contains 30,728 records and was sourced in June 2026. * According to the listing, the exposed information includes:   * First Names   * Last Names   * Professional Contact Numbers   * Email Addresses   * Membership Information   * Titles/Civility Fields * Sample records published by the actor appear to reference personnel affiliated with French healthcare institutions. * The dataset was reportedly released publicly rather than being offered exclusively for sale.  Analyst Note: While the claimed record count is relatively modest compared to large-scale consumer breaches, exposure of healthcare-sector contact databases can facilitate targeted phishing, business email compromise (BEC), credential harvesting, and social engineering campaigns against hospitals and public health organizations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇲🇽 RENAPO Database Allegedly Offered for Sale – 70+ Million Records Claimed  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database belonging to Mexico's National Population Registry (RENAPO).  * The seller states the dataset contains records spanning all 32 Mexican states, 320 municipalities, and 17 database tables. * According to the listing, the exposed information allegedly includes:   * CURP identifiers   * Personal information   * Residential addresses   * Birth and death records   * Marriage and divorce records   * Naturalization records   * Identity documents   * Family relationship data   * Biometric-related records   * Historical CURP information   * Digitized documents   * Access logs and administrative data * The actor claims the database contains more than 70 million records and is offering samples to potential buyers.  Analyst Note: If authentic, this would represent one of the most significant government identity-related exposures in Mexico, potentially impacting a substantial portion of the population. The combination of identity records, civil registry data, family relationships, and biometric information would present serious long-term privacy, fraud, and national security concerns.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

📝 Notion User Database Allegedly Offered for Sale – 110 Million Records Claimed  A threat actor is advertising a dataset allegedly linked to Notion, claiming more than 110 million user records are available for sale.  * The listing describes Notion as the target and includes a sample allegedly containing:   * Email addresses   * Email verification status   * User names   * Password hashes   * Signup IP addresses   * Last login IP addresses   * Account creation and activity timestamps   * Locale, timezone, and country information   * Workspace names and roles   * Subscription and trial information * The authenticity of the dataset and its relationship to Notion infrastructure remain unverified. * No public evidence has been presented confirming the data originated from a recent compromise of Notion systems.  Analyst Note: Claims involving large SaaS platforms frequently attract significant attention, but volume alone should not be interpreted as proof of a breach. Validation of data freshness, source attribution, and whether records originate from production systems, third-party integrations, historical leaks, or aggregation activity is essential before assessing impact.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Notion

🇷🇴 SmartBill Allegedly Breached – 82,510 Records Exposed  A threat actor is advertising a dataset allegedly associated with SmartBill, a Romanian invoicing and business management platform.  * The listing claims to contain 82,510 records. * According to the threat actor, the exposed data includes:   * First Names   * Last Names   * Phone Numbers   * Email Addresses   * CNPs (Romanian national identification numbers)   * Physical Addresses   * Banking Details * Sample records were provided to support the claim, though the authenticity and source of the dataset remain unverified.  Analyst Note: The inclusion of CNP numbers and banking information significantly elevates the potential impact of this exposure. Such data can facilitate identity theft, financial fraud, account takeover attempts, and highly targeted phishing campaigns against affected individuals and businesses.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Romania

🌍 TripAdvisor Database Allegedly Offered for Sale – 30 Million Records Claimed  A threat actor is advertising a dataset allegedly linked to TripAdvisor, claiming approximately 30 million records are available for sale.  * The forum post includes sample data and marketing material intended to support the claim. * Based on the visible sample, the dataset appears to contain user-related information, though the full scope and authenticity remain unverified. * TripAdvisor is one of the world's largest travel platforms, serving millions of travelers, hotels, restaurants, and tourism providers globally. * No evidence has been presented publicly confirming whether the data originates from TripAdvisor systems, third-party partners, scraping activity, or previously exposed datasets.  Analyst Note: Large consumer datasets tied to travel platforms frequently reappear on cybercrime forums, often mixing newly obtained records with historical breaches or scraped data. Verification of data freshness and origin is critical before assessing the true impact of such claims.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #TripAdvisor

sample_tweets.text: ⚠️🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 110M Notion User Records on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post alleges the sale of a database containing over 110 million unique user records from Notion, the popular all-in-one workspace application. The listing, published on June 23, 2026, claims to offer comprehensive account data including email addresses, password hashes, IP addresses, and subscription details.  According to the post, the dataset includes the following fields for each record: *   id *   email *   email_verified *   name *   password_hash (appearing as bcrypt hashes in samples) *   signup_ip *   last_login_ip *   created_at *   last_active_at *   locale *   timezone *   country *   workspace_name *   workspace_role *   plan *   subscription_status *   vat_id *   trial_ends_at *   canceled_at  The seller provided sample entries showing users from various countries, including the US, Canada, Brazil, Argentina, India, France, Italy, Spain, Australia, and the UK. Some samples include corporate email domains such as @lumeninc.com, @helixdesigne.com, and @quantahr.com, alongside personal providers like Gmail, Outlook, and Yahoo. The listed timestamps in the samples range from 2020 to mid-2026.  Notion is a widely used productivity tool with millions of users globally, employed by individuals and enterprises for note-taking, project management, and collaboration. If authentic, the exposure of hashed passwords and login IPs could facilitate credential stuffing attacks or targeted phishing campaigns against high-value accounts.  At the time of reporting, Notion has not issued a public statement regarding this alleged breach, and the incident has not been independently verified.

🚨 Argentina 76 Million Telephone Records Leaked on Dark Web  A dark web marketplace is currently offering a dataset containing 76 million telephone records from Argentina for $5,000. The leak includes personally identifiable information (PII) such as full names, mobile phone numbers, residential addresses, localities, provinces, and postal codes. Given Argentina's population of approximately 45 million, the 76 million records suggest a comprehensive database that may include duplicates, business lines, or historical entries. The data appears to be structured similarly to official telecommunications or civil registries. This massive exposure poses significant privacy risks and could be exploited for phishing, smishing, identity theft, and targeted marketing campaigns. Security experts recommend that individuals in Argentina monitor their accounts for suspicious activity and remain vigilant against unsolicited communications.  #DataLeak #Argentina #Telecommunications #DarkWeb #PII #ThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of Indian Insurance Policy Data on Darkweb Forum  A darkweb forum post claims to offer Indian insurance policyholder data for sale, though the listing title references "Stakebet Casino" while the sample data appears to be from a life insurance provider.  According to the underground forum listing, the actor alleges possession of comprehensive policy records containing sensitive personally identifiable information. The sample data provided in the post reveals detailed insurance policy information including:  - Full customer names and residential addresses - Mobile phone numbers and email addresses - PAN (Permanent Account Number) tax identifiers - Birth dates and age information - Policy numbers, premium amounts, and sum assured values - Nominee names and relationships - Product names including "AADHAAR STAMBH," "AADHAAR SHILA," "JEEVAN UMANG," "JEEVAN LAKSHYA," "JEEVAN LABH," and "NEW ENDOWMENT"  The product names in the sample data correspond to policies offered by the Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC), the world's largest life insurer by number of policyholders. LIC is a government-owned corporation with over 300 million active policies and serves as a critical financial institution in India.  The data sample shows policyholders from the Jalgaon district in Maharashtra, with complete contact details, financial information, and government-issued tax identifiers exposed. If authentic, exposure of such comprehensive policy data could enable identity theft, financial fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns against millions of Indian citizens.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and LIC has not issued any public statement regarding a data compromise. The discrepancy between the listing title referencing a casino platform and the insurance data shown raises questions about the accuracy of the threat actor's claims.  #DataBreach #DataSale #Insurance #India #LIC #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 2 Million Chinese Bank Loan Customer Records Leaked on Telegram  A database containing approximately 2 million unique records of loan customers from an unnamed Chinese bank has been leaked on a Telegram channel. The breach, posted by user @hongmenxing on June 19, 2026, exposes highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) alongside detailed financial data.  The compromised dataset includes fields such as full names, gender, mobile phone numbers, dates of birth, Chinese ID card numbers, contact addresses, and education levels. Additionally, it reveals critical financial information, including monthly income, monthly debt repayment amounts, mortgage status, and marital status. The combination of Chinese national ID numbers with financial profiles significantly elevates the risk of identity theft, targeted phishing campaigns, and financial fraud.  Chinese ID card numbers are notoriously difficult to change and are widely used for authentication across various services. When paired with income and debt data, this information can be exploited by threat actors for credential stuffing, social engineering, or selling on dark web markets. The affected financial institution has not yet issued a public statement regarding the breach.  Security researchers advise individuals in China to monitor their credit reports and remain vigilant against suspicious communications. The leak underscores the ongoing challenges in securing large-scale financial databases against unauthorized access and data exfiltration.  #DataBreach #ChinaCyber #BankingSecurity #PII #FinancialData #ThreatIntelligence #DarkWeb

🚨 40 Million Indian Female Phone Numbers and PII Database Leaked on Dark Web  A massive database containing approximately 40 million records of Indian female phone numbers has been exposed on a dark web forum, raising significant privacy and security concerns. The leaked dataset includes highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), such as full names, mobile numbers, email addresses, physical addresses, cities, states, professional categories, and gender. The breach was announced in a forum thread dated June 19, 2026, with a sample of the data provided to verify its authenticity.  The sample entries reveal detailed personal and professional information, with records primarily concentrated in Delhi and surrounding regions. Fields in the dataset include names alongside corresponding mobile numbers, Gmail and Yahoo email addresses, and specific residential or business addresses. The data also categorizes individuals by industry sectors such as banking, retail, engineering, and hospitality. A direct download link via a file-sharing service and a Telegram handle were provided by the anonymous poster, indicating the data is being actively distributed or sold.  The scale of this leak is substantial, encompassing tens of millions of individuals. Exposure of this volume of PII creates severe risks for targeted phishing campaigns, spam calls, social engineering attacks, and identity theft. For the affected individuals in India, the breach underscores critical vulnerabilities in data protection practices and highlights the ongoing threat of large-scale credential and PII harvesting on underground markets. Security experts warn that such datasets are frequently cross-referenced with other breaches to build comprehensive profiles for malicious actors.  #DataLeak #IndiaPrivacy #PIIBreach #DarkWeb #CyberSecurity #IndianData #PrivacyBreach

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Breach of Latvia Companies Registry Database  A post on an underground forum claims that a threat actor operating under the alias REALWHITEHATHACKER12 has obtained a database from the Republic of Latvia’s official Companies Registry (ur.gov.lv). The actor alleges the data was accessed following a configuration error in the registry's infrastructure.  According to the listing, the leaked dataset contains corporate and personal information tied to registered entities in Latvia. The exposed fields reportedly include: - id - uri - at_legal_entity_registration_number - entity_type - position - governing_body - name - latvian_identity_number_masked - birth_date - legal_entity_registration_number - rights_of_representation_type - representation_with_at_least - registered_on - last_modified_at  The Latvian Enterprise Registry is a central government database that maintains records for all legal entities and business operators in the country. If authentic, the exposure of corporate ownership structures, board member details, and partially masked national ID numbers could facilitate corporate espionage, targeted phishing campaigns, and identity-related risks for business leaders.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of the Latvian Companies Registry has not been independently verified.  #DataLeak #Latvia #GovernmentBreach #CompaniesRegistry #PII #DarkWeb #CyberSecurity

🚨 150,000+ Student and Faculty Records Leaked from University of Mustansiriyah in Iraq  A threat actor has claimed to leak over 150,000 records belonging to students and faculty members from the University of Mustansiriyah in Iraq. The compromised dataset reportedly includes highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) such as full names, dates of birth, national ID numbers, family numbers, email addresses, phone numbers, and physical addresses. Additional academic details like college names, sections, stages, and admission types are also included.  The University of Mustansiriyah, established in 1987, is one of the largest public universities in Baghdad and a major educational institution in Iraq. The breach of 150,000+ records poses significant privacy and security risks for the affected individuals, particularly due to the inclusion of government-issued ID numbers and family identifiers commonly used in Iraq.  The leak was posted on an underground forum with a demand for payment, though the identity of the threat actor remains unknown. Authorities and the university administration have not yet issued an official statement regarding the incident.  #DataLeak #UniversityOfMustansiriyah #EducationSecurity #IraqCyber #PIIBreach #ThreatIntelligence #DarkWeb

🚨RHODES Claims Massive Data Breach of Russian Defense Institute VNIIR-M  A darkweb forum post reveals that a threat actor calling themselves RHODES claims to have exfiltrated approximately 116GB of sensitive data from VNIIR-M, a Russian scientific research institute specializing in radio communications and electronic defense.  According to the listing on an underground forum, the alleged breach encompasses 125,000 files containing highly sensitive defense-related information. The post claims the leaked data includes:  - Military research and development activities - Invoices and contractual agreements - Organizational structures including executive leadership and governance frameworks - Commercial information on partners including addresses, telephone numbers, and email contacts - Component sourcing and supply chain management records - Inventory of components used in Russian military systems - Projects involving the integration of weapon systems  RHODES, a known threat actor group, allegedly offered a 10GB free sample of the data to demonstrate authenticity. The group claims to be selling exclusive access to the full dataset for $100,000, with non-exclusive access priced at $60,000.  VNIIR-M is a Russian scientific research institute responsible for advancing Russia's electronic sovereignty, including secure communications and defense-related research and development. The institute plays a critical role in Russia's military technology infrastructure, particularly in radio communications and electronic defense systems.  If authentic, the exposure of military component sourcing, weapon system integration projects, and defense R&D activities could have significant implications for national security and defense intelligence. The alleged breach targets a key institution in Russia's military technology supply chain.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of VNIIR-M has not been independently verified, and the authenticity of the claimed data remains unconfirmed.  #DataBreach #RHODES #DefenseSecurity #RussianMilitary #DataSale #CyberEspionage #ThreatIntelligence

sample_tweets.text: AryStinger is a previously undocumented Linux botnet that hijacks neglected, end-of-life routers and NAS devices, turning them into a covert reconnaissance-and-proxy network. Unlike typical DDoS or cryptomining botnets, it’s purpose-built for the pre-intrusion phase - each infected device becomes an “Executor” that scans the internet, fingerprints services, enumerates subdomains, tunnels traffic, and runs operator commands, all while the device keeps working normally so the infection goes unnoticed.  Key points:  •Two variants - a stripped C build (RTL819X) for legacy D-Link/Linksys routers, and a fuller Go build (Standard) for QNAP NAS with intranet scanning and Go/Java/Python code execution. •Three n-day CVEs - CVE-2013-3307 (Linksys) and CVE-2016-5681 (D-Link) for routers; CVE-2025-11837 (QNAP Malware Remover) for NAS. •Controller/Executor model - bots authenticate, get a unique Executor ID, and receive shards of a larger scan job for parallel, distributed reconnaissance. •Obfuscated C2 - Protobuf + XOR (Go adds gzip) over HTTP/HTTPS, keyed with sh_#@!_2024_secret. Persistence via Dropbear SSH on TCP/2332 (routers) or gs-netcat (NAS). •Scale & geography - 4,300+ routers confirmed (still rising), led by South Korea (48.45%) and China (31.82%); D-Link DIR-850L is ~75% of devices. NAS scale unmeasured. •Low detection, no attribution - initial samples were 0/VirusTotal; tradecraft mirrors state-aligned ORB networks, but XLab makes no attribution. •Main risk - the device becomes hidden attack infrastructure inside a trusted perimeter. EoL routers can’t be patched, so replacement is the only durable fix.

A person operating under the moniker "mizanthropiaz" compromised the Brazilian governments Emergency Alert System in Sao Paulo, Rio, and Brasilia. The Threat Actor sent a notification to hundreds of thousands of people which read "misanthropi4".  More details have emerged regarding the compromise, and truthfully, it is terrifying. The Brazilian government is extremely secure and it is quite remarkable any living person could have gotten past their enhanced security measures.  "mizanthropiaz" found a username and password login to the Brazilian governments Emergency Alert System. The username and password was present because an employee working there accidentally infected themselves with malware in 2016.  The employee there did not change the password in over 10 years.  But, after the employee accidentally infected their computer with malware, did they change their password? No.  But, did the Brazilian government do IP address blacklisting which would prevent unauthorized devices from accessing the Emergency Alert System? No.  But, did the Brazilian government require a VPN connection to authenticate to the Emergency Alert System because it's a government network? No.  But, did the Brazilian government require MFA (e-mail, text, or Authenticator Code)? No.  But, did the Brazilian government send notifications on new devices connecting? No.  But, did the Brazilian government issue alerts on password changes or password change requests? No.  But, did the Brazilian government require e-mail verification prior to changing or resetting passwords? No.  But, did the Brazilian government have rate-limiting to prevent brute-force attacks? No.  But, did the Brazilian government introduce a CAPTCHA to prevent brute-force attacks? Yes, but it was 2+2 and it never changed. It always asked "2+2="  But, did the Brazilian government require password complexity to make brute-forcing difficult? No, the password to the Brazilian government employee was the same as their username.

This should remove a large part of the historical BYOVD driver pool on systems that actually reach enforcement. Good move.  But WHCP signing is still not a security review. At @nextronresearch, we regularly find WHCP-signed drivers with obvious privilege-escalation flaws. Microsoft now controls the trust choke point and should use it for automated vulnerability screening - static and dynamic analysis, dangerous IOCTL checks, maybe LLM-assisted triage before signing.  As Michael points out here, reaching enforcement isn’t automatic. A cross-signed driver that repeatedly loads and would fail the new policy can keep resetting the evaluation period, potentially preventing the system from ever reaching enforcement.

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI 에이전트의 취약점을 악용하거나, AI를 활용해 정교한 공급망 공격 및 자동화된 인증 우회 공격을 수행하는 추세입니다. 특히 Cisco Unified CM과 같은 엔터프라이즈 인프라의 취약점을 즉각적으로 악용하는 사례가 빈번하며, 클라우드 버킷 하이재킹 및 OAuth 토큰 탈취를 통한 데이터 유출이 주요 위협으로 부상했습니다. 또한, 오픈소스 생태계와 CI/CD 파이프라인을 겨냥한 악성 패키지 배포 및 스피어 피싱이 지속적으로 고도화되고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Scattered Spider**: Transport for London을 대상으로 한 대규모 사이버 공격 수행, 최근 영국 법원에서 유죄 판결.
*   **Gentlemen**: EDR killer 프레임워크를 유지하며 금융권 및 IT 기업을 타겟팅하는 랜섬웨어 그룹.
*   **FishMonger**: SprySOCKS for Windows 백도어를 활용하여 커널 드라이버 수준의 은밀한 공격 수행.
*   **Kimsuky**: 보안 담당자를 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격을 통해 개인정보 유출 문의를 미끼로 악성코드 유포.
*   **KongTuke**: Mistic 백도어를 활용해 보험, 교육, IT 분야를 타겟팅하는 랜섬웨어 초기 침투 브로커(IAB).

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **스피어에이엑스(SpearAX)**: 미국 반도체 기업 Blaize와 엣지 AI 컴퓨팅 협력을 위한 MOU 체결 (공급망 보안 관점의 모니터링 필요).
*   **한-일 AI 비즈니스 대화**: 한국정보산업연합회와 일본 JISA 간 AI 협력 논의 (기술 교류에 따른 보안 가이드라인 준수 필요).
*   **Kimsuky**: 국내 보안 담당자를 노린 스피어 피싱 공격 지속 확인.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-20230**: Cisco Unified CM, Critical(CVSS 9.1), 현재 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-55200**: libssh2, Critical(CVSS 9.2), RCE 취약점.
*   **CVE-2026-41089**: Windows Netlogon, RCE 취약점.
*   **CVE-2026-47729**: Squid Proxy, 29년 된 메모리 누수 취약점(Squidbleed).
*   **CVE-2026-45504**: Microsoft Exchange, 패치 권고.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE**:
    *   CVE-2026-20230 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/cisco-unified-cm-flaw-exploited-after.html))
    *   CVE-2024-40766 ([SANS ISC](https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/33094))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Cisco Unified CM 패치**: CVE-2026-20230 취약점에 대해 즉시 최신 보안 패치를 적용하고 WebDialer 설정을 점검하십시오.
*   **공급망 보안 강화**: npm 등 오픈소스 패키지 사용 시 의존성 분석을 수행하고, 알려진 악성 패키지(예: `chai-as-init`)가 포함되어 있는지 확인하십시오.
*   **클라우드 설정 검토**: 클라우드 버킷의 권한 설정을 재검토하여 글로벌 네임스페이스 하이재킹 위험을 차단하고, OAuth 토큰 사용 이력을 모니터링하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8/10
*   **근거**: Cisco 인프라 취약점의 즉각적인 악용과 AI 에이전트를 겨냥한 새로운 공격 기법이 동시에 발견되어 기업 보안 환경에 높은 위험을 초래함.
